repo_purpose: Shinobi: autonomous Windows-native AI agent for Spanish YouTubers that researches, transcribes, and analyzes video formats to generate localization briefs, packaged as a single executable.
architecture_summary:
## Overview
Shinobi is a TypeScript-based autonomous agent targeting Windows, built on Node.js with ESNext/NodeNext module resolution and packaged via Node SEA (Single Executable Application) into `shinobi.exe`.

## Core Layers
- **Root**: Build orchestration (`build_sea.mjs`), project metadata (`package.json`), and TypeScript config.
- **src/**: Core utilities including GitHub release watchers (Hermes), a PowerShell-based ESC kill-switch for aborting screen actions, path/permission validation, and command execution via `child_process.execSync`.
- **scripts/**: Smoke tests and runner scripts for the reader's `/read` and `/self` endpoints, producing validation markdown artifacts.
- **missions/**: Intended to hold mission configuration and runtime logic (unverified — scan timeouts).
- **docs/**, **misc/**: Not readable in this pass.

## External Stack
Playwright (browser automation), better-sqlite3 + sqlite-vec (local vector memory), OpenAI SDK, @nut-tree-fork/nut-js (desktop automation), axios, dotenv. Build tooling: esbuild, tsx, ts-node, typescript.

## Coverage Gaps
The root README references sub-modules (coordinator, tools, cloud, bridge, db, memory, persistence, skills, notifications) that were not individually scanned in the provided sub-reports, so internal architecture beyond src/utils and src/watchers is inferred only from the root's dependency list.

risks.description: missions/ folder unreadable: subagent timed out at 90s on all attempts. Core mission logic entirely unverified — major architecture gap.
docs/ not read — validation failed twice on key_files schema; documentation coverage gap.
misc/ not read — validation failed twice on key_files schema; coverage gap.
License contradiction: root package.json declares both MIT and ISC fields; README confirms ISC. Ambiguous license per root sub-report.
Test suite not implemented at root (npm test exits 1), yet README marks B11 E2E complete and test_b*.ts files are referenced but absent per root report.
src/utils/runner.ts uses execSync with no timeout, no input sanitization, and no privilege checks — command injection / blocking risk.
src/utils/kill_switch.ts embeds hard-coded PowerShell Win32 API calls; brittle to PS execution policy and Windows-only.
src/utils/permissions.ts falls back WORKSPACE_ROOT to cwd with no symlink resolution; path traversal defenses may be bypassable in production.
src/watchers/hermes_watcher.ts hardcodes NousResearch/hermes-agent repo and lacks 429/5xx retry or rate-limit handling.
scripts/a4_run.ts hardcodes Windows paths (C:\Users\angel\...) limiting portability; silently swallows JSON parse errors.
scripts/b1_smoke.ts uses cache-busting dynamic import query string indicating module caching / test isolation issue.
No lock file visible at root per sub-report; npm install resolves semver ranges non-deterministically.
